what_it_does: Renders a modal tool palette that lists available tools, shows a selected tool's human description, builds a schema-driven options form, validates required inputs, invokes a generic /api/tools/invoke endpoint, and displays honest run-status messages while holding raw results for a future DNA tool-card renderer.
observations: Modal overlay with scrim and Escape-to-close/back behavior.
Tool list derives a human title from name/title and shows description.
Seeds form values from schema defaults or first enum value via defaultsFor.
RunState is a small state machine: idle, running, done, gated, error.
Required-field validation is op-conditional using tool.opField and tool.opRequired.
POSTs {name, args} to /api/tools/invoke and reads the tool output from data.result.
Non-safe posture tools are blocked in the UI and show a 'gated' message instead of firing.
Result region only shows human status/count; it stores hits but does not render them as raw JSON.
Inline comments explicitly mark pending seams for the invoke endpoint and DNA.renderArchetype integration.
standouts: Explicit 'honest pending' UX: no fake results and no raw JSON.
UI-side safety gate that refuses to fire non-safe/mutating tools.
Generic /api/tools/invoke integration rather than tool-specific endpoints.
Op-conditional required-field validation tied to the selected operation.
conventions: Uses React hooks (useState, useEffect) and a store hook (useTools).
Functional component with small inner handler closures.
Accessibility attributes on the dialog and live result region.
Inline comments serve as both design rationale and seam markers.
concerns: All invocation errors collapse into the same generic user message, losing diagnostic detail.
Raw result hits are stored in state but never surfaced, leaving data in UI state unused.
The selected-tool useEffect suppresses the exhaustive-deps lint rule.
The /api/tools/invoke URL and posture values are hard-coded assumptions.
The 'gated' state has no retry/approval flow within this file.
notes: A modal tool-runner that keeps the operator experience simple and safe by listing tools, driving a form from schema, invoking generically, and deliberately showing honest pending or gated status instead of raw output.
questions: What is the full ToolDescriptor contract for opField, opRequired, labels, and posture?
What input types and behaviors does SchemaForm implement?
How is the operator-token approval gate (#1b) intended to be wired?
What is DNA.renderArchetype and when will it write into the result container?
What posture values does the server expose beyond 'safe'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is a focused, non-developer tool palette with a live list, form, run action, and an intentionally pending result render region.
intention_signals: It is trying to give non-technical operators a plain-language way to pick a tool, set its options, run it safely, and see a human status while the system finishes designing rich result rendering.
novelty: The deliberate 'honest pending' pattern: storing result hits but only rendering a count/status, with explicit comments naming unlanded seams rather than faking or raw-dumping them.
contribution: A reusable modal tool-runner arrangement that separates tool listing, schema-driven form validation, generic invocation, and result staging, plus a UI-side posture gate and a human-readable status container that a fused system could keep.
summary_for_embedding: ToolsPanel.tsx is a React modal that lists tools, renders a schema-driven form, invokes /api/tools/invoke, and displays honest run-status, pending, gated, or error messages while holding results for future DNA rendering.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to give operators a simple, safe, plain-language tool palette without exposing raw developer output.

symbols.does: Formats a tool descriptor into a human-readable title.
symbols.description: Returns the tool's title if present, otherwise transforms the name by replacing underscores and hyphens with spaces and title-casing each word.

symbols.does: Builds initial form values from a tool's JSON schema defaults.
symbols.description: Iterates over the tool inputSchema properties and seeds values from prop.default or the first enum entry so the form opens ready to run.

symbols.does: Describes the possible run-status states of a tool invocation.
symbols.description: A discriminated union covering idle, running, done with a held hit list and count, gated for unsafe tools, and error with a human message.

symbols.does: Main React component for the modal tool palette.
symbols.description: Renders the overlay, tool list, selected tool detail, schema form, run button, and honest result region; handles loading, errors, Escape key, and invocation state.

symbols.does: Invokes the selected tool via the generic API endpoint.
symbols.description: Blocks non-safe posture tools with a gated state, POSTs {name, args} to /api/tools/invoke, parses the envelope, and stores the result hits with a human count/status or an error message.

symbols.does: Handles Escape key navigation inside the modal.
symbols.description: When the panel is open, pressing Escape returns to the tool list if a tool is selected, otherwise closes the panel.

symbols.does: Updates a single form field value.
symbols.description: A small setter wrapper that merges the new field value into the existing values state immutably.
